title: Example GitHub Actions CI step documented for connector
description: Write a ready-to-copy GitHub Actions workflow snippet demonstrating how a consuming team wires the connector's `build` command and uploads the artifact to their SystemBook instance via `/api/previews`.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation file (e.g. `docs/ci-example.md` or embedded in TASK-61's connector docs) contains a complete, copy-pasteable GitHub Actions job

Example installs Node.js and the connector package, runs the build command, and does an authenticated `curl`/`fetch` POST to `/api/previews` per built variant with `component_name`, `variant_id`, `commit_sha` (using `github.sha`)
Example references the upload token as a GitHub Actions secret (e.g. `${{ secrets.SYSTEMBOOK_UPLOAD_TOKEN }}`), never a hardcoded value
Example triggers on push to the default branch (documented as the recommended trigger, adjustable by the consuming team)
A short prose explanation precedes the YAML explaining what each step does and how to obtain the upload token (pointing to TASK-44's admin screen)

steps.details: Write a `.github/workflows/systembook-preview.yml` example: checkout, setup-node, `npm install`/`pnpm install`, run `npx systembook-connector build --root . --out .systembook/dist`.

Add a step that iterates built variant directories and performs an authenticated multipart POST to `https://<instance-url>/api/previews` with the required fields, using `curl -F` or a small inline script.
Add prose explaining how to generate a token via TASK-44's admin screen and add it as a repository secret named `SYSTEMBOOK_UPLOAD_TOKEN`, plus how to set `SYSTEMBOOK_INSTANCE_URL`.

Reference this example from TASK-60's setup docs and TASK-61's `*.preview.tsx` docs, so a new team following the main setup guide is pointed here at the right step. [Cross-links registrados como pendência: os docs das TASK-60/61 ainda não existem — ao criá-los, apontar para docs/ci-example.md; o próprio ci-example.md já referencia os dois no texto.]

technicalNotes: This is a documentation artifact, not code executed by SystemBook itself — no automated verification beyond a human/CI review of the YAML for correctness against the actual `connector build` CLI flags implemented in TASK-41.
